answer: A user would pay for both 5-minute and 1-hour cache writes in the same request when both 1-hour and 5-minute cache controls are included in the request, with the 1-hour cache entry appearing before any 5-minute cache entries. The token count is split as follows: cache read tokens are charged for the highest cache hit position (A); 1-hour cache write tokens are charged for the token count between position A and the highest 1-hour cache_control block after A (B - A); and 5-minute cache write tokens are charged for the token count between the highest 1-hour cache_control block and the last cache_control block (C - B).

On the third turn, with each turn adding 15 new blocks and only one explicit breakpoint used, the lookback window checks at most 20 positions (blocks 35 through 16). If the previous cache write was at block 15 (from turn 2), it is one position outside the window, so there is no cache hit. The system does not find a matching entry, and you do not get a cache read for earlier content.

Claude Opus 4 (and Claude Opus 4.1 and Claude Opus 3) has the highest cost difference between its base input tokens ($15 / MTok) and cache hits ($1.50 / MTok), with a numerical difference of $13.50 per million tokens.
Enabling web search modifies the system prompt, which invalidates the system and messages caches but does not invalidate the tools cache.

cache_read_input_tokens: 8,000
cache_creation_input_tokens: 1,000
input_tokens: 200
total input token count: 9,200
